title: Install Alipay and complete real-name verification
description: Alipay is essential for QR payments at shops, taxis, and attractions.
steps: Download Alipay before departure and switch language to English in settings.
Add at least one Visa or Mastercard and complete identity verification.
Test a small payment when you arrive to confirm card binding works.
verification_note: App availability and setup flow based on official Alipay resources.

title: Set up WeChat for messaging and backup payments
description: Many services communicate through WeChat, and some merchants prefer WeChat Pay.
steps: Create or recover your account before departure.
Enable payment options if available in your region.
Save key hotel and guide contacts in WeChat for easy access.
verification_note: Feature availability varies by account region and bank support.

title: Use Amap (Gaode Maps) for most in-China navigation
description: Amap is usually more reliable than global map apps for local transit and addresses.
steps: Download Amap before departure.
Save hotel names in Chinese and pin railway stations you will use.
Use metro routing mode to avoid peak-hour car congestion.
verification_note: Amap functionality confirmed through official product site and typical traveler usage.

title: Use Dianping for restaurant discovery and wait times
description: Dianping helps with local restaurant rankings, photos, and peak-hour line expectations.
steps: Download Dianping and enable location.
Translate restaurant names to Chinese for easier map matching.
Check recency of reviews before committing to a venue.
verification_note: Dianping is a primary local dining discovery platform.

title: Add Didi for ride-hailing backup
description: Useful when metro is inconvenient or for airport/rail transfers with luggage.
steps: Install Didi and add international card options where supported.
Save your hotel names in both English and Chinese.
Confirm pickup points in large rail stations to avoid confusion.
verification_note: Payment compatibility can differ by country-issued cards.

title: Carry two payment methods (digital + card + small cash)
description: Digital wallets dominate, but backup card/cash helps when terminals or account links fail.
steps: Keep one backup Visa or Mastercard separate from your primary card.
Carry small RMB notes for edge cases and markets.
Enable travel notices or app alerts for anti-fraud blocks.

title: Set up eSIM or roaming before arrival
description: Activation is easier before travel and avoids airport setup friction.
steps: Install and activate your eSIM before flight departure.
Save QR and activation instructions offline.
Keep one secondary connectivity option for redundancy.
verification_note: Carrier compatibility and performance vary by plan and device.

title: Understand internet restrictions and app availability
description: Some common US services may not be available without alternatives.
steps: Prepare local alternatives for maps, translation, and communications.
Download any work-critical files before travel.
Confirm your company policy before using any VPN service.
verification_note: Policies and technical behavior can change; verify close to departure.

title: Use 12306 or Trip.com for high-speed rail tickets
description: Rail is often the fastest intercity option (e.g., Chengdu to Chongqing).
steps: Create account and verify passport details before travel.
Book priority trains 1-2 weeks early in peak seasons.
Arrive 45-60 minutes early for security and gate procedures.
verification_note: 12306 is the official rail booking platform.

title: Plan airport and rail transfer buffers
description: Urban congestion and station size can cause delays if transfers are too tight.
steps: Add 60-90 minute transfer buffers in major cities.
Use metro during peak traffic windows when practical.
Keep hotel name cards in Chinese for drivers and station staff.
verification_note: Timing ranges are practical planning guidance.

title: Reserve high-demand meals and carry Chinese venue names
description: Popular restaurants can have long queues and name translation issues.
steps: Book in advance where possible via concierge or phone.
Save venue names in Chinese text and screenshot directions.
Check opening days and lunch/dinner service windows.
verification_note: Operational details vary by restaurant and season.

title: Use respectful dining and temple etiquette
description: Small etiquette signals improve interactions and avoid awkward moments.
steps: Avoid placing chopsticks upright in rice bowls.
Use indoor voice levels in tea houses and temples.
Ask before photographing people in intimate settings.
verification_note: Etiquette is regionally nuanced; guidance is general-purpose.

title: Save emergency contacts and embassy details offline
description: Offline access is crucial if connectivity degrades.
steps: Store local emergency numbers and your hotel front desk number.
Save the nearest US embassy/consulate contacts.
Keep passport photo page and visa docs in secure offline storage.
verification_note: US citizen services guidance sourced from US Embassy resources.

title: Use travel insurance that includes medical and interruption
description: Coverage is valuable for medical events and schedule disruptions.
steps: Confirm policy includes emergency evacuation and trip interruption.
Keep insurer hotlines and policy numbers in cloud + offline notes.
Understand pre-authorization requirements before you need care.
verification_note: Aligned with official US travel health risk guidance.

title: First 48 hours checklist
description: A practical startup sequence to reduce friction after landing.
steps: Activate data, test Alipay/WeChat payment, and pin hotel in Amap.
Withdraw small RMB backup cash and confirm onward rail tickets.
Run one trial metro ride and one ride-hail to verify app setup.
verification_note: Operational checklist assembled from standard traveler workflows.
